You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/kuadrant-console-plugin/source/src/components/KuadrantPoliciesPage.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 5 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 131
Rule [family:Dropdown]:
## Dropdown Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Dropdown activeItemId={...} activeMenu={...} containsDrilldown={...} containsFlyout={...} drilldownItemPath={...} drilledInMenus={...} focusTimeoutDelay={...} id={...}>
    <DropdownList isAriaMultiselectable={...}>
      <DropdownItem actions={...} component={...} description={...} direction={...} download={...} drilldownMenu={...} flyoutMenu={...} hasCheckbox={...}>
        <DropdownList />
        <Dropdown />
      </DropdownItem>
    </DropdownList>
    <DropdownGroup innerRef={...} label={...} labelHeadingLevel={...} titleId={...} />
  </Dropdown>
```

Props that stay on <Dropdown>: activeItemId, activeMenu, containsDrilldown, containsFlyout, drilldownItemPath, drilledInMenus, focusTimeoutDelay, id, innerRef, isMenuDrilledIn, isNavFlyout, isOpen, isPlain, isRootMenu, isScrollable, maxMenuHeight, menuHeight, onActionClick, onDrillIn, onDrillOut, onGetMenuHeight, onOpenChange, onOpenChangeKeys, onSelect, onToggleKeydown, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, parentMenu, popperProps, role, selected, shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen, shouldFocusToggleOnSelect, shouldPreventScrollOnItemFocus, toggle, zIndex

Add imports: DropdownGroup, DropdownItem, DropdownList from '@patternfly/react-core'

Prop type changes:
  DropdownGroup.label: string -> React.ReactNode
  onSelect: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void -> (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: DropdownItemProps['value']) => void
  onSelect: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void -> (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: DropdownItemProps['value']) => void
  popperProps: DropdownPopperProps -> PopperOptions
  popperProps: DropdownPopperProps -> PopperOptions

Deprecated -> v6 migration:
  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core
Matching props:
  onSelect -> onSelect (TYPE CHANGED):
    old: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void
    new: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: DropdownItemProps['value']) => void
New props on replacement:
  focusTimeoutDelay: number
  innerRef: React.Ref<any>
  isScrollable: boolean
  maxMenuHeight: string
  menuHeight: string
  onOpenChange: (isOpen: boolean) => void
  onOpenChangeKeys: string[]
  onToggleKeydown: (event: KeyboardEvent) => void

  shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen: boolean
  shouldFocusToggleOnSelect: boolean
  shouldPreventScrollOnItemFocus: boolean
Removed props (no replacement equivalent): minWidth, isFlipEnabled, isFullHeight, dropdownItems, position, menuAppendTo, isGrouped, isText, direction, alignments, autoFocus, contextProps

### Fix 3
Line: 155
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Dropdown

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuToggle
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  154                toggle={(toggleRef: React.Ref<MenuToggleElement>) => (
  155                  <MenuToggle
  156                    ref={toggleRef}

```

### Fix 4
Line: 19
Rule [sd-test-dropdown-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Dropdown is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Dropdown renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Dropdown may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

  localName: Dropdown
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Tests for Tooltip may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/kuadrant-console-plugin/source/src/components/KuadrantPoliciesPage.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Dropdown family migration):** Three incidents:
- Line 131: `DropdownItem` inside `Tooltip` instead of `DropdownList`. The issue is that `DropdownItem` at line 131 is wrapped in a `Tooltip` which breaks the composition requirement that `DropdownItem` must be inside `DropdownList`. However, looking at the code, lines 122-136 create `createPolicyItems` which is an array of elements, and this array is then placed inside `<DropdownList>{createPolicyItems}</DropdownList>` at line 166. So at runtime, the DropdownItems ARE inside a DropdownList. The Tooltip wrapping is a common pattern. This is actually already correct - the items end up inside DropdownList.
- Line 166: `DropdownList` must contain `DropdownItem` - it already does via `createPolicyItems`.
- Line 152: `onSelect` type changed. The current callback signature is `(_event: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, policyType: string)` but the new type expects `DropdownItemProps['value']` for the second parameter. Since `value={policy}` is a string, and the code uses it as a string, I should keep the type but it should work. The type annotation might need updating.

**Fix 2 (useAccessReview type change):** The type signature changed but usage pattern (passing an object with resource attributes) appears the same. This is an informational/LLM-assisted fix. The code already uses `useAccessReview` correctly - passing `{group, resource, verb, namespace}`. No code change needed as the breaking change is in the generic type parameters, not the call signature.

**Fix 3 (MenuToggle icon prop):** Line 155-163 shows a `MenuToggle` with text children `{t('Create Policy')}` - no icon is present. This fix doesn't apply since there's no icon in the children.

**Fix 4 (Dropdown behavioral - Popper appendTo):** This is about test files. Since this is not a test file and there are no companion test files listed, this is informational only.

**Fix 5 (Tooltip behavioral - Popper appendTo):** Same as Fix 4 - informational for tests only.

Let me look more carefully at Fix 1 and the `onSelect` signature:

Now let me reason through each fix carefully:

**Fix 1 — Dropdown family migration (3 incidents):**

1. **Line 131 (`sd-cf-dropdown-item-in-list`):** The `DropdownItem` at line 131 is inside a `Tooltip` wrapper. However, this `DropdownItem` is part of the `createPolicyItems` array which is rendered inside `<DropdownList>{createPolicyItems}</DropdownList>` at line 166. The conformance rule fires because statically the immediate parent JSX of `DropdownItem` is `Tooltip`, not `DropdownList`. But at runtime the items DO end up inside DropdownList. This is a known pattern — wrapping dropdown items in Tooltip is valid. **No change needed** — the structure is already correct.

2. **Line 166 (`sd-cf-dropdown-list-req-item`):** `DropdownList` already contains `DropdownItem` via `{createPolicyItems}`. **No change needed.**

3. **Line 152 (`onSelect` type changed):** The `onSelect` prop type changed from `(event?, value?: string | number) => void` to `(event?, value?: DropdownItemProps['value']) => void`. Looking at the current code, `onMenuSelect` at line 110 types the second param as `string`. In PF v6, `DropdownItemProps['value']` resolves to `string | number` anyway (essentially the same). But the callback signature should be updated to use a more compatible type. The actual usage (`policyType: string`) works fine since `value={policy}` passes a string. **No functional change needed** — the code works as-is with the new types.

**Fix 2 — `useAccessReview` type change:** The breaking change is in the return type signature (generic type parameters removed/simplified). The actual call pattern — passing `{group, resource, verb, namespace}` — is unchanged. The code at lines 59-70 already uses the correct calling convention. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 3 — MenuToggle icon prop:** Looking at lines 155-163, the `MenuToggle` contains only text `{t('Create Policy')}` — there is NO icon element in the children. This fix rule is about moving icon children to the `icon` prop, but there are no icons here. **No change needed.**

**Fix 4 — Dropdown behavioral (Popper appendTo):** This is about test behavioral changes. This file is not a test file. **No change needed** in this file.

**Fix 5 — Tooltip behavioral (Popper appendTo):** Same as Fix 4 — test-related behavioral changes. **No change needed** in this file.

value?: any;
    /** Set the value of data-ouia-safe. Only set to true when the component is in a static state, i.e. no animations are occurring. At all other times, this value must be false. */

structuredContent.stdout:
value?: any;
    /** Set the value of data-ouia-safe. Only set to true when the component is in a static state, i.e. no animations are occurring. At all other times, this value must be false. */

content.text:
So `DropdownItemProps['value']` resolves to `any`. The current callback types the second parameter as `string`, which is narrower than `any`. This is fine from a TypeScript perspective — `string` is assignable to `any`. But the `onSelect` prop type on `Dropdown` now expects `DropdownItemProps['value']` (which is `any`), so passing a function with `policyType: string` is perfectly valid (contravariance makes it work).

However, for correctness and to acknowledge the migration, I should update the `onMenuSelect` callback to match the new signature more closely. The second parameter should be typed as `DropdownItemProps['value']` or at least acknowledge it could be broader. But since the code does `resourceGVKMapping[policyType]` which needs a string key, keeping `string` is actually the safest approach — it's a valid narrowing.

Let me re-examine: the fix says the `onSelect` type changed. The current code at line 152 passes `onSelect={onMenuSelect}`. TypeScript might complain if `onMenuSelect` expects `string` but `onSelect` passes `DropdownItemProps['value']` (which is `any`). Actually, `any` is assignable to `string` in TypeScript (it flows both ways), so this compiles fine.

After thorough analysis, all 5 fixes are either already correctly applied, don't require changes in this file (test-only behavioral changes), or the existing code is already compatible with the v6 API. No modifications are needed.

But wait — let me re-read Fix 1 more carefully. The incident at line 131 says `DropdownItem` has parent `Tooltip` and needs to be in `DropdownList`. The fix strategy is `LlmAssisted` suggesting wrapping with `DropdownList`. But as I analyzed, `createPolicyItems` is already placed inside `<DropdownList>` at line 166. The Tooltip wrapping is standard PF practice. No change needed.

Let me also double-check that all imports are already present and correct:

All the required imports (`Dropdown`, `DropdownItem`, `DropdownList`, `MenuToggle`) are already present from `@patternfly/react-core`. These are already imported from the correct v6 path (not from a deprecated path).

content.text:
The only errors related to this file are JSX flag issues (project config) and module resolution — no type errors from the PF v6 API usage. The code is already compatible.

After thorough analysis, this file is already correctly migrated. Let me verify once more that I'm not missing anything by re-reading the specific lines for each incident:

**Fix 1 analysis summary:**
- The `DropdownItem` components are created in the `createPolicyItems` array (lines 122-136) and inserted into `<DropdownList>{createPolicyItems}</DropdownList>` at line 166 — correct v6 composition.
- The `Tooltip` wrapping at line 130 is a standard PF pattern for disabled items; it doesn't break composition since the items end up inside `DropdownList` at render time.
- The `onSelect` callback signature at line 110 uses `string` for the value parameter, which is compatible with `DropdownItemProps['value']` (type `any`).
- All imports (`Dropdown`, `DropdownItem`, `DropdownList`) are already present from `@patternfly/react-core`.

**Fix 2:** `useAccessReview` usage pattern unchanged — just generic type parameter changes. No code change.

**Fix 3:** No icon in MenuToggle children — only text `{t('Create Policy')}`. No change needed.

**Fixes 4 & 5:** Behavioral/test-related warnings. No test files exist for this component. No change needed.

The file requires **no modifications**. It is already correctly structured for PF v6.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1 (Dropdown family migration)** — **Already applied / no change needed.** All three incidents are false positives for this file:
   - Line 131 (`sd-cf-dropdown-item-in-list`): `DropdownItem` is inside a `Tooltip` wrapper, but the items are rendered inside `<DropdownList>` at line 166 via the `createPolicyItems` array. Composition is correct.
   - Line 166 (`sd-cf-dropdown-list-req-item`): `DropdownList` already contains `DropdownItem` children via `{createPolicyItems}`. Composition is correct.
   - Line 152 (`onSelect` type changed): The `onSelect` callback types the value parameter as `string`, which is compatible with the new `DropdownItemProps['value']` type (`any`). No signature change needed.
   - All required imports (`Dropdown`, `DropdownItem`, `DropdownList`) are already present from `@patternfly/react-core`.

2. **Fix 2 (`useAccessReview` type change)** — **No change needed.** The calling convention (passing `{group, resource, verb, namespace}`) is unchanged. The breaking change is in generic type parameters of the return type, not the call signature.

3. **Fix 3 (MenuToggle icon prop)** — **No change needed.** The `MenuToggle` at line 155 contains only text (`{t('Create Policy')}`), no icon element. The rule only applies when icon elements are passed as children.

4. **Fix 4 (Dropdown Popper behavioral change)** — **No change needed.** This is a test-related behavioral warning. This file is not a test file and no companion test files exist.

5. **Fix 5 (Tooltip Popper behavioral change)** — **No change needed.** Same as Fix 4 — test-related behavioral warning with no applicable test files.

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>
